title: ipv6_addr.h struct in6_addr fallback may conflict with system headers
description:
## Location
firmware/bridge-zephyr/subsys/lichen/ipv6_addr.h:76-78

## Finding
The fallback definition of `struct in6_addr` relies on a complex cascade of preprocessor checks to detect whether the system already provides this struct. The heuristics (checking for `IN6ADDR_ANY_INIT`, header guards like `_NETINET_IN_H`, `__APPLE__` signals) are fragile.

## What goes wrong
If a platform provides `struct in6_addr` via a header that none of the checked macros detect, the fallback definition triggers a "redefinition of struct" compile error. This is a maintenance burden as new platforms are added.

## Mitigation already present
The code documents this and provides `LICHEN_HAVE_IN6_ADDR` as an escape hatch. The current heuristics cover common targets (POSIX, Zephyr, macOS).

## Recommendation
Low priority - the existing documentation and escape hatch are adequate. Consider adding a comment pointing to the specific platforms tested (e.g., "verified on: Zephyr 3.x, Linux glibc, macOS 14").
close_reason: Added summary table comment explaining the struct in6_addr detection cascade, including explicit guidance for conflict resolution
